items.title: Monitoring started: Knox County Registry of Deeds

page_lines: A tax of $2.20 per $500 in value for sales up to $1,000,000 and a tax of $6 per $500 in value for the portion exceeding $1,000,000 is imposed upon the transferring of real estate unless exempt pursuant to Title 36, §4641-C. Maine Revenue Service Property Tax
provides information about Real Estate Transfer Tax.
* State and Municipal Government Submitters: $25 to record each document
* All other submitters: $40 to record each document
, documents that relate to real estate (property) ownership are recorded and searched. The purpose of the Registry of Deeds is to
all land records in Knox County. Documents are accepted for recording based on Maine State Law (see below)
. The Registry is administered by the Registrar of Deeds and is responsible for recording documents as prescribed by these statutes. View our
for more information and to sign up for fraud alerts.
pertaining to duties of the Registry of Deeds
Our major purpose is to make available, for all time, the information that proves ownership and interest in every piece of property in Knox County.
date back to 1760, which include the records of Lincoln, Waldo and Hancock Counties before Knox County was incorporated on April 1, 1860.

Documents such as deeds, mortgages, liens, discharges, assignments and certain court documents, just to name a few, are entered into public record in the order they are received. They are made available to the public through a paper and electronic index. The Registry is used every day by many individuals; banks, credit bureaus, lawyers, title companies, genealogists and independent researchers.
The Registry of Deeds office is not permitted, by law, to give legal advice.
We recommend that you consult with an attorney for all legal matters. The Registry of Deeds office is not permitted, by law, to give legal advice.
How do I add or change a name on my deed?
You need to prepare a new deed. We suggest that you consult a lawyer to prepare your documents.
How do I find a survey plan of my land?
If filed, you can search on our website (see
I had a lien against me which has now been paid, but it is showing up on my credit report. Can you take it off?
No, the Registry does not originate any records here. We cannot release any liens without a proper written release or discharge.
I want to research a piece of property. What information do I need to start?
are indexed under the property owner's name. If you do not know the owner's name, you can contact the town office in which the property is located.
